abstract: This paper develops a case for integrating rhetorical and ecological frameworks to study digital image memes and their role in disseminating online narratives. Building on the DRE3 model, we analyze how memes function as rhetorical artifacts that shape public discourse through parsimony, polysemy, and identity construction in digital public spheres.
